issues.claim: All agent runs have complete standard artifacts including result.json
issues.expected_evidence: result.json files present for all agents in these scenarios
issues.actual_evidence: Missing result.json for legacy runs; verdict is supported by verify.log and metrics instead
issues.recommendation: Accept as legacy artifact gap, or rerun BB-001 and BB-003 to match the standard artifact schema.

issues.claim: All token/cost values have per-row telemetry provenance
issues.expected_evidence: telemetry_strategy and telemetry_source populated for every agent run with cost data
issues.actual_evidence: Strategy and source are null for BB-001 and BB-003; they are legacy published metrics without row-level provenance
issues.recommendation: Accept legacy caveat as documented, or rerun the scenarios to capture full telemetry.

issues.claim: Token breakdowns sum exactly to total tokens
issues.location: docs/site-data.json (OpenCode runs in BB-002, BB-004 through BB-012)
issues.expected_evidence: Sum of token_breakdown components exactly equals the total tokens field
issues.actual_evidence: Slight mismatches (e.g. breakdown sum 105836 vs total 105968) due to unexposed provider accounting categories
issues.recommendation: The limitation is already documented in docs/site-data.json limitations; no code fix required.

issues.claim: All telemetry evidence is fully raw-replayable from committed files
issues.expected_evidence: Committed raw agent logs or telemetry databases for all rows claiming token costs
issues.actual_evidence: Several runs reference private/local paths (e.g., local MiMoCode SQLite store or local ccusage sessions) that are not committed, categorized as *_not_raw_committed
issues.recommendation: Provide sanitized JSON exports of these local telemetry databases within the repository to allow full third-party validation without leaking private paths.

methodology_notes: Dashboard correctly claims 12 scenarios, 72 runs, 70 passes, and 2 fails (verified in site-data.json and raw runs).
Fastest, cheapest, and best execution claims are correctly aggregated from the underlying wall-clock, cost, and process scores.
Execution quality is fully transparent and explicitly excludes token/cost efficiency, which are evaluated separately.
Normalized public cost is clearly separated from vendorCost throughout the dataset.
The UI is careful not to overclaim, explicitly pointing users to the audit pack for provenance caveats instead of claiming fully raw-backed telemetry for all rows.
